items.title: Teen dies after crashing bike at Canberra's Stromlo Forest Park

children.content: Police say the 14-year-old boy came off his bike while riding in Stromlo Forest Park on Saturday, and later died in hospital.

image.altText: A photo of the forest park with shrubbery, rocks and signs and blue sky in the distance.

items.title: Reefs at risk as warming water sees sea urchins spread further

children.content: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

image.altText: diver under water with sea urchins

items.title: Varroa mite 'in all corners' of ACT as more than 1,000 beehives destroyed

children.content: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

gallery.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

thumbnail.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

items.title: 'Grow up': Meta censoring warnings about illicit drugs, health experts say

children.content: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

image.altText: a small orange and grey pill next to a ruler

items.title: Man jailed for role in major steroid and prescription drug syndicate

children.content: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

image.altText: A box of pills and drugs.

items.title: Mary has fallen into an aged care 'black hole' and sees no way out

children.content: After being stuck in a hospital in Canberra for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

gallery.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody on war crimes charges

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail and a court adjourned his criminal case until June.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's fight to clear his name just got more perilous

children.content: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

gallery.altText: a man wearing a suit arriving at a court and looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: a man wearing a suit arriving at a court and looking at the camera

items.title: Trump and Vance wade into Hungarian election to save far right Orban

children.content: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

image.altText: Viktor Orbán and JD Vance wave at a political rally in Hungary

items.title: First person charged with 'from the river to the sea' offence faces court

children.content: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

image.altText: A man speaks in front of several microphones

images.altText: A printscreen of photoshop with a woman and a Christmas tree

children.content: Are you finding it increasingly difficult to know what to believe online? Hamish Macdonald and our panel of experts are helping us unpack misinformation, disinformation, and AI, and how you can identify them.

items.title: Can you pick the real from the fake? These experiments might surprise you

updates.title: Keeping your kids safe from misinformation
Discerning the truth from a lie

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: From 'anger and hate' to feeling 'lighter', Jack is back to being herself

children.content: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — "lighter" and with more personal belief than ever.

image.altText: Shayna Jack smiles

items.title: Smallest town in Australia for sale as two residents decide to sell roadhouse

children.content: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

items.title: ASX enjoys $80 billion rally, oil falls back below $US100 on US-Iran ceasefire — as it happened

children.content: The news of a US-Iran ceasefire in the Perisan Gulf sparked a big rally on Asian share markets, with the ASX up almost 3 per cent while oil prices plummeted.

image.altText: 3D printed oil barrels and rising stock graph

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

image.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: What is financial stress, and how does it affect our decisions?

children.content: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

gallery.altText: Lady holding a wallet with 20 dollar bills.

thumbnail.altText: Lady holding a wallet with 20 dollar bills.

items.title: 'I just had to do it for me': The ambiguous loss of family estrangement

children.content: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

image.altText: A man sitting on a rock looking out towards the ocean

items.title: Quiz: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

image.altText: Elvis Presley impersonators gather for the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of Elvis Presley impersonators.

items.title: Cost of filling best-selling car up by $100 since start of Iran war

children.content: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

gallery.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

thumbnail.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

items.title: Rideshare driver accused of rape and stalking multiple women

children.content: A Brisbane rideshare driver had been charged with rape, stalking and coercive control over incidents involving three women across the city over the span of five years.

image.altText: Traffic at night.

items.title: Body found on rural Victorian property during search for missing man

children.content: Police have discovered the body of a man on a property east of Ouyen in regional Victoria.

image.altText: image of richard

items.title: Parents found guilty of starving daughter win appeal, granted bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

image.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.

items.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't reflect modern society

children.content: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

image.altText: A smiling young woman stands next to a glass cabinet of historical objects.

items.title: Where cattle once roamed, conservation now grows — and so do concerns

children.content: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

items.title: Matt Canavan looks to stars for 'Australia on steroids' plan

children.content: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

image.altText: Matt Canavan, smiling and wearing a suit and tie, walks down a corridor at parliament house.

items.title: 'A worker is going to be stabbed': Union airs hospital safety concerns

children.content: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

image.altText: Staff wearing masks in emergency department at hospital

items.title: Vehicle rollover on WA mine site kills worker, seriously injures another

children.content: A 38-year-old man dies in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

image.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Golfer details PTSD, says he 'thought people were trying to kill me'

children.content: In an interview before this week's US Masters, former US Open champion Gary Woodland opens up about his mental health struggles.

items.title: Growing frustration in real estate industry over Qld seller disclosure laws

children.content: Real estate experts say Queensland's seller disclosure laws, which were introduced in August, are fuelling 'gazumping' and leading to unnecessary costs.

image.altText: A red sold sticker across a for sale sign outside a house with a large front yard on a sunny day

items.title: Babies, as young as three months, kidnapped and sold overseas

children.content: Babies, some as young as three months, were allegedly sold to people in Singapore and across Indonesia.

image.altText: Close-up photo of baby feet laying on a white blanket.

items.title: Why this photo shoot made rugby star Mel Kawa feel like a fraud

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

image.altText: Five women's Super Rugby captains pose for a photos with young girls, al with their hands on their hips.

items.title: Husband's dying wish for more kids an impossible choice in childcare desert

children.content: In the depths of grief, this family wants to grow. But a childcare crisis stands in its way.
